column1: # knob-prose-baseline.tsv — BURN-DOWN DEBT LEDGER for check-knob-prose.sh
# (temperloop#164/#169, item registry-config-lints), the prose counterpart to
# workflows/scripts/kernel/personal-token-denylist-baseline.tsv (same
# mechanism, same consumed-once semantics — see that file's header for the
# exemption-vs-baseline distinction it inherits):
#   * the `<!-- knob-prose:allow -->` per-line marker is for PERMANENT,
#     legitimately-literal prose (a doc line that must show a value);
#   * THIS file is for PRE-EXISTING violations of the D3 "prose names knobs,
#     never values" rule that are debt, not exceptions — every row here is
#     meant to be fixed and removed. The follow-up item
#     (prose-tunables-migration) rewrites each listed line to name-only
#     (dropping the restated default) and deletes its baseline row — the
#     goal is an EMPTY file, not a maintained one. Do not add new rows: fix
#     new violations at source (or, if genuinely legitimate, mark the line
#     with the allow-marker), don't grandfather them in.
# Format: <repo-relative path> TAB <knob name> TAB <registry default,
#         verbatim> TAB <exact matched line content>
# Matching (check-knob-prose.sh): a hit is suppressed only when its (file,
# name, default, line-content) tuple exactly matches a row here, and each
# row suppresses exactly ONE occurrence — so a genuinely NEW violation
# anywhere, including a new duplicate of an already-listed line, still fails
# the gate. A row whose line no longer matches (already fixed, or the file
# changed around it) simply goes unused — expected burn-down progress, not
# an error.
# ── EMPTY (prose-tunables-migration, temperloop#164/#169) ──────────────────
# All 9 pre-existing rows this file once carried (8 in claude/commands/
# build.md, 1 in sweep.md — all BUILD_QUOTA_PAUSE_PCT / BUILD_MERGE_GATE_
# WINDOW / BUILD_QUEUE_TIMEOUT / BUILD_MERGE_BACKEND / BUILD_HEADLESS_POLL_
# TIMEOUT restatements) were rewritten to name-only prose and removed. Kept
# as a zero-row file (not deleted) so check-knob-prose.sh's `-f` guard and
# this header's burn-down history stay visible; a future genuinely
# pre-existing violation would be baselined here the same way, but new
# violations should be fixed at source instead (see above).
